key_insight: IGA-ODIL replaces neural-network PINN parameterizations with smooth B-spline bases and robust residual minimization, making PDE training faster, sparser, and better conditioned.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical route to faster forward and inverse PDE solvers for engineering simulation, especially where PINNs are too slow or unstable.
method: Represent the unknown field with isogeometric B-spline basis functions instead of a neural network.
Minimize a robust weighted residual loss derived from Gram operators so the loss tracks true error better.
Solve the resulting sparse structured system with efficient Gauss--Newton optimization.
evidence_signal: The paper evaluates Poisson, advection--diffusion, Helmholtz, Allen--Cahn, and inverse Helmholtz benchmarks and reports orders-of-magnitude speedups over PINNs and CRVPINNs while keeping high accuracy.
watch_item: Performance depends on spline discretization, problem conditioning, and Gauss--Newton setup, so difficult geometries or very large 3D systems may still require careful tuning.
paper_title: IGA-ODIL: Optimizing DIscretre robust Loss with Isogeometric Analysis to solve forward and inverse problems faster using machine learning tools
